stance: Risk-aware flexi-cap: keep the high-conviction quality/cyclical core, tilt toward names with strong/rising analyst revisions, and prune the clearly-falling-revision laggards while adding liquid large/mid ballast to temper a hot beta.
experience_note: I am watching whether the analyst-revision tilt actually adds information ratio over the next 1-3 months: the names I am adding/keeping for strong revisions (ONGC, LICI, MUTHOOTFIN, COFORGE, GUJGASLTD, GLAND, EQUITASBNK) should, as a basket, outperform the falling-revision names I am exiting (ADANIPORTS, HINDPETRO, LT, SRF, SUMICHEM, MFSL). If that revision spread does NOT show up - i.e. the exited basket holds up as well as the added basket over a fair common window - then ARM added no transfer here and I am over-trusting a weak (IC~0.05) signal; I would cut the tilt and revert toward the liquid-core baseline. I am also watching realized beta: if the prune of small-cap cyclicals does not bring beta below ~1.15 while excess return holds, the risk reduction is illusory. Pre-registered so this is judged without hindsight.
